question: What's the best time to visit Toowoomba QLD with kids?
answer: Spring (September-November) is absolutely stunning when the Carnival of Flowers is on, but can get busy! Autumn's also lovely with mild weather perfect for young ones. Winter (June-August) can be chilly, so pack warm layers. Summer's great for outdoor fun, though afternoon storms are common. School holidays mean more activities but higher prices and crowds. Mid-week visits outside school hols offer the best value and quieter parks.

question: Are there pet-friendly holiday parks in Toowoomba QLD?
answer: You bet! Several holiday parks around Toowoomba welcome furry family members. Most pet-friendly parks require dogs to be leashed, up-to-date with vaccinations, and may have designated pet areas. Some charge a small additional fee (usually $10-20 per night). Always ring ahead to confirm their pet policy and book a pet-friendly cabin or site. Remember to bring your pet's bedding, bowls, and waste bags!

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Toowoomba QLD during school holidays?
answer: School holiday rates typically range from $120-250 per night for cabins sleeping 4-6 people, and $45-70 for powered sites. Prices peak during Queensland school holidays and long weekends. Book early (3-6 months ahead) for better rates and availability. Off-peak weekdays can be 30-40% cheaper. Many parks offer stay-3-pay-2 deals outside peak times. Budget extra for activities, park entry fees, and meals out.

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Don't forget: sunscreen, hats, insect repellent, and first-aid kit! Pack comfy clothes for all weather, swimmers, and closed-toe shoes. Bring favourite toys, books, and devices for downtime. Most cabins have kitchens, so consider bringing basics like cereal, snacks, and coffee. BBQ tools if you're camping. Portable high chair, night light, and comfort items help littlies settle. Torch, bikes, and outdoor games are brilliant additions!

question: What is there to do near Toowoomba QLD holiday parks for families?
answer: Heaps to keep the kids entertained! Visit Cobb+Co Museum, Ju Raku En Japanese Garden, and Picnic Point for stunning views. The Toowoomba Regional Botanic Gardens has great playgrounds. Queens Park is perfect for picnics and feeding ducks. Nearby Crows Nest has amazing waterfalls. Many holiday parks have jumping pillows, playgrounds, and pools on-site. The Empire Theatre often has family shows too!
